name: Jambalaya Frank's Cajun
recipeIngredient: 16 oz Rotel tomatoes and hot peppers
recipeInstructions:
To make a really good pot of jambalaya, you're going to need a well-seasoned black cast iron Dutch oven with a tight-fitting lid. An 8-quart pot is perfect for this recipe.
1. Put the pot on the burner over high heat and pour in the bacon drippings (or margarine).
2. Add the smoked sausage, andouille, tasso, and crumbled bacon to the pot. Stir-fry the meats until the smoked sausage turns light brown, which should take about 8 minutes.
3. Toss in the julienned chicken and stir-fry it until every strip loses its translucency (turns white).
4. Add the onions, bell pepper, celery, and garlic to the pot. Reduce the heat to medium-high and cook the vegetables until they soften.
5. Pour in the rice and stir thoroughly into the seasoning vegetables and meats so that every single grain is moistened for about 4 to 5 minutes.
6. Add the tomatoes, beef stock, Kitchen Bouquet, thyme, chili powder, and blend everything together well. Bring the mixture to a slow boil.
7. Taste the liquids and season the dish with salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper to taste. Season it a little on the "heavy side" because the rice will absorb much of the seasonings as it cooks, and you still have a couple pounds of shrimp to mix in. You can add Tabasco for flavor.
8. Reduce the heat as low as possible, put the lid on the pot, and let the jambalaya simmer for about an hour. This slow-cooking process allows each grain of rice to cook evenly, puff properly, and absorb the flavors.
9. About 5 minutes before serving, stir in the raw shrimp, green onions, and parsley. Put the lid back on the pot and continue to simmer over low heat until the shrimp turn pink.
Warnings:
- Do not remove the cover from the pot during the slow-cooking process. Releasing steam will hinder cooking the rice, causing it to be hard in the center and the jambalaya to be dry.
- If your stove-top cannot cook the jambalaya slowly enough, mix the liquids, put the lid on the pot, set the oven to 300°F, place the pot in the oven, and bake for about 45 minutes. The dish will come out perfect.
Note: This recipe is from "Frank Davis Cooks Naturally N'Awlins" by Frank Davis.
